questions: OQ-2 (from brief): Does `<URL>` wrapping defeat Discord's same-channel collapse to channel-pill? Discord's `<URL>` syntax suppresses rich embeds/unfurling, and the channel-pill collapse is a form of unfurling, so it should work. If it doesn't in practice, the fallback is to render each citation as `[N] <message_id>` on its own line without a clickable URL — we'd then need to revisit. This can only be verified with a live Discord test after deployment.

Are there other functions besides `_dispatch_understand_media` that download media via `requests.get` and could produce similar rejection errors? If so, should they also be enriched with source metadata? (The brief scopes to understand_video, but if there's a shared helper it might be worth enriching there too.)

success_criteria.criterion: Rejection lines in admin embed include a clickable Discord jump URL when message_id/channel_id/guild_id are present in the outcome
Rejection lines in admin embed include `media_url:` line when media_url is present in the outcome
Rejection rendering falls back gracefully to today's single-line format when source metadata fields are missing (no crash, no empty lines)
Per-block citation URLs in render_topic_publish_units are wrapped in `<...>` so Discord suppresses same-channel collapse
All existing tests in tests/test_topic_editor_runtime.py pass
New tests cover rejection field jump URL rendering and citation angle-bracket wrapping
No regression in admin-embed field layout (same field order, names, conditional rendering)
Documentation note at _dispatch_understand_media captures the URL truncation investigation conclusion
URL validation guard at line 1314 prefers proxy_url when url doesn't start with http
Live Discord test confirms `<URL>` wrapping prevents same-channel collapse to channel-pill (manual verification after deploy)

assumptions: Discord's `<URL>` syntax suppresses both rich embed unfurling AND same-channel message-link collapse to `#channel-name`. This is the standard documented behavior (wrapping in angle brackets suppresses all link previews). If it doesn't work for the specific channel-pill collapse, the fallback noted in OQ-2 will be needed.

The `guild_id` available in `updates` at `_build_trace_embed` (line 2621 — `"guild_id": self._resolve_guild_id()`) is sufficient for constructing jump URLs. Channel-level specificity comes from the enriched outcome.

`_dispatch_understand_media` is the only code path producing the user-reported rejection. If other tool handlers also download media (e.g., `understand_image` uses the same `_dispatch_understand_media` with `media_kind="image"`), they are covered by the same fix. No other handlers use `requests.get` for media downloads.

The bare filename `LTX-23-i2v_00416-audio.mp4` in the 404 error is likely an archive data artifact where the stored attachment `url` field is just a filename. The validation guard + `media_url:` surface line is sufficient mitigation without a database migration.
The 1024-char embed field limit is sufficient for the enriched rejection format (jump URL + media_url + error). Multiple rejections are truncated as today.
